steps.title: Clear the decimal equation
steps.content: Start with $-0.9y=0.8x+2.2$. Multiply by $-10$ to avoid decimals: $$9y=-8x-22,$$ or $$8x+9y=-22.$$

steps.title: Rewrite the second equation
steps.content: The second equation is $4y=-3x+18$, which can be written as $$3x+4y=18.$$

steps.title: Eliminate y
steps.content: Multiply $8x+9y=-22$ by $4$ to get $32x+36y=-88$. Multiply $3x+4y=18$ by $9$ to get $27x+36y=162$. Subtract the second from the first: $$5x=-250.$$ Thus $x=-50$.

steps.title: Check with Desmos
steps.content: Graphing the two original lines gives an intersection with $x=-50$, which is exactly what the question asks for. The answer is -50.
